items.correct: Self-aware metafiction, intertextuality, scepticism toward grand narratives, playfulness with form, and blurring of high/low culture.
items.misconception: Postmodernism just means literature written after modernism.
items.random_wrong: Postmodernism means writing about the future.
items.why: Postmodernism is a specific set of attitudes and techniques, not a date range. A novel written in 2024 may be entirely traditional and not postmodern in any meaningful sense.

items.q: What is the structure of a Shakespearean sonnet?
items.correct: Fourteen lines in iambic pentameter, structured as three quatrains and a final couplet, rhyming ABAB CDCD EFEF GG, often with a volta near the couplet.
items.misconception: A sonnet is any short love poem.
items.random_wrong: A sonnet must always be exactly ten lines long.
items.why: Students treat sonnet as a vague genre. It is a strict 14-line form with specific rhyme schemes — and sonnets need not be about love (think Hopkins's religious sonnets).

items.q: What is the function of dystopian fiction?
items.correct: To critique tendencies in the writer's own society by extrapolating them into a nightmarish future — dystopias hold up a warning mirror to the present.
items.misconception: Dystopian fiction is just storytelling set in scary futures.
items.random_wrong: Dystopian fiction predicts what will actually happen in 100 years.
items.why: Dystopias are about the present, not the future. 1984 critiques 1948; The Handmaid's Tale critiques 1980s America. Students miss the satirical function and read them as prophecy.

items.q: Which is NOT a good axis for comparison?
items.correct: A theme so broad it fits any text
items.why: Correct answer: A theme so broad it fits any text. “A specific narrative technique” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.
It's Parallel summary. “Specific evidence” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Minimum number of texts for comparative study?
items.why: The real figure is 2. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
